title: Blue Green Cutover Checkpointing
description: A release-and-rollout control workflow that manages promotion waves, rollback readiness, and deployment dependencies across environments. Focus case: blue green cutover checkpointing.
assumptions: Target slots, endpoints, and dependency inventory are pre‑provisioned and discoverable by the checkpoint controller.
Backups and snapshotting are atomic and available for any deployment slot used during the checkpoint.
Approvals and runbooks are modeled as explicit artifacts and must be acquired to progress promotion gates.
Traffic policies and load balancer changes are reversible and support staged (percentage) traffic shifts.
Automation scripts are deterministic and idempotent when invoked by the checkpoint workflow.
risks: Dependency unavailability: required downstream services or external dependencies may be missing or degraded, blocking promotion.
Configuration drift: configuration items attached to a checkpoint may diverge from production expectations, causing failures.
Insufficient validation coverage: verification suites may not detect regressions introduced during cutover.
Rollback gaps: snapshot or backup inconsistencies can prevent reliable rollback to pre‑cutover state.
Approval bottlenecks: manual approval gates can delay promotion and extend exposure windows.
Traffic policy misconfiguration: incorrect traffic policy application can route traffic prematurely or incompletely.
